_instructions: STEP 1 — run the proxy (downloads phi-4-mini ~2.5 GB on first run):
STEP 2 — add the chatLanguageModels entry to:
(create the file if it does not exist — the value is a JSON array)
STEP 3 — add the settings.json block to your VS Code user settings.json:
Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P) → Preferences: Open User Settings (JSON)
STEP 4 — reload VS Code (Ctrl+Shift+P → Reload Window)
IMPORTANT: Do NOT use 'copilot-utility-small' as a BYOK model ID.
That string is VS Code's internal model name. Using it as a BYOK model ID
creates a circular lookup that VS Code cannot resolve.
Use 'phi-4-mini' for both chat.utilityModel and chat.utilitySmallModel.
_block1_chatLanguageModels_comment: Paste this object into your chatLanguageModels.json array.
The file is a JSON array: [ { ...existing... }, { paste here } ]
_block2_settings_json_comment: Add the three keys below to your VS Code settings.json.
All three are required for Agent mode to work with BYOK.
chat.utilityModel and chat.utilitySmallModel must match your BYOK model ID,
